metadata.title: The Case of the Vanished Spring Feast
metadata.one_liner: The grand elderberry cake and candied truffles have disappeared from the Town Hall!

visual.style: storybook watercolor with gentle fantasy detail
visual.palette: elderberry purple, forest green, and polished gold

cover.summary: A feast mystery with a torn window, missing cake, and a hidden tree stash.

narrative.premise: Brunilia's Spring Feast disappeared from the Town Hall kitchen just before the celebration. Use the clues to expose the thief.
starting_knowledge: The feast vanished from the Town Hall Kitchen.
The thief must have slipped in during a short gap.

locations.name: Town Hall Kitchen
locations.description: A polished kitchen with an empty cake stand and a torn window screen.
search_context: Gray fur clings to the torn screen.
A shiny blue button rests near the stand.

visual.summary: A grand kitchen with marble counters, an empty cake stand, and a torn screen.

locations.name: The Babbling Brook
locations.description: A clear stream with smooth stones and muddy banks.
search_context: Fresh paw prints mark the bank.
A pile of washed berries sits on a cloth.

visual.summary: A sparkling brook with stones, berries, and muddy paw prints.

locations.name: The Great Oak Tree
locations.description: An ancient oak with a hollow trunk used for storage.
search_context: Purple elderberry stains lead up the bark.
A truffle wrapper is hidden by the roots.

visual.summary: A giant oak tree with a hollow trunk and berry-stained bark.

roleplay.persona: A fast-talking squirrel who defends himself too quickly.
roleplay.background: He is famous for hoarding food.

characters.private_alibi: I was organizing my acorn collection all morning.
characters.private_motive: He wanted the feast for his private stash.

visual.summary: A gray squirrel in a green vest missing a blue button.

roleplay.persona: A confident raccoon who notices lots of details.
roleplay.background: She was washing berries for the feast.

characters.private_alibi: I've been at the brook washing berries.
characters.private_motive: She loves sweets and seems suspicious at first.

visual.summary: A sleek raccoon with a purple ribbon and berry basket.

roleplay.persona: A dramatic chef who speaks proudly about his cooking.
roleplay.background: He spent days preparing the feast.

characters.private_alibi: I went to the cellar for flour.

visual.summary: A sturdy badger chef in a crisp hat and spotless tunic.

evidence.player_text: The kitchen window screen is torn wide enough for a squirrel to squeeze through.
evidence.fact_summary: The thief entered through the window.

evidence.player_text: A shiny blue button lies on the kitchen floor.
evidence.fact_summary: The button matches Silas's damaged vest.

evidence.player_text: Penelope's washed berries show she stayed by the brook.
evidence.fact_summary: Penelope has a strong innocent alibi.

evidence.player_text: Silas slips and mentions finding something special for his stash.

ground_truth.what_happened: Silas tore through the kitchen screen and stole the feast to hide it in the Great Oak Tree.
ground_truth.why_it_happened: He wanted the feast for his famous food stash.
ground_truth.explanation: Silas lost a blue button at the kitchen window and carried the feast to his tree while Penelope stayed at the brook and Chef Badger was in the cellar.

suspect_truths.actual_activity: He entered through the kitchen window and stole the feast.
suspect_truths.stated_alibi: I was organizing my acorn collection all morning.
suspect_truths.motive: He wanted the feast for his stash.

suspect_truths.actual_activity: She washed berries by the brook.
suspect_truths.stated_alibi: I've been at the brook washing berries.
suspect_truths.motive: She likes sweets but did not take the feast.

suspect_truths.actual_activity: He fetched flour from the cellar.
suspect_truths.stated_alibi: I went to the cellar for flour.

timeline.summary: Chef Badger goes to the cellar for flour.
Silas tears the kitchen screen and steals the feast.
Silas hides the feast inside the Great Oak Tree.
